behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure mode is in request construction logic within a single module — the module forgets or truncates the taxonomy label list when building the API payload. This bug is visible at a single function boundary. MOCK-HIDE: Mocking the API and capturing call arguments is exactly the right tool; the mock does not hide this failure. REAL-INFRA: No OS/network dependency needed. LLM-DEP: We are asserting on the outbound request structure, not the response quality. A Unit test that mocks the API client and asserts that every expected label appears in the captured call arguments is sufficient and complete.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the classification module with a known taxonomy list. Mock the external API client. Call classify() with any document. Capture the arguments the mock was called with. Assert that every label in the taxonomy appears in the prompt or structured labels field of the API request.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure mode is entirely within the module's response-validation branch — a missing or wrong guard after receiving the API response. This is one function's post-processing logic. MOCK-HIDE: Mocking the API to return an out-of-taxonomy string (e.g., 'marketing') is precisely how we exercise the guard; the mock does not hide the failure. REAL-INFRA: No. LLM-DEP: No — we are injecting a controlled bad response; LLM quality is irrelevant. A Unit test fully covers this.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the external API to return a label string that is not in the taxonomy (e.g., 'marketing'). Call classify() with any document. Assert the return value is exactly "unknown". Repeat with several out-of-taxonomy strings to confirm the guard is general, not hard-coded to one case.

behaviors.description: The classification result for the same document is the same across repeated calls.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This behavior is not about module wiring — it is an assertion about real LLM output stability. MOCK-HIDE: A mocked API is deterministic by construction and will always return the same value, making this test trivially pass regardless of actual LLM behavior. The failure mode — a real LLM returning different labels on different calls for the same input — is completely invisible without a real model call. LLM-DEP: YES. Running the same input 100 times against a real AI/ML API can yield different results due to temperature, sampling, or model updates. The correctness criterion here is stability of a real model output, which is inherently Agentic. MULTI-STEP: No — a single call per trial is enough.
behaviors.test_description: Call classify() with the same representative document N times (e.g., 5–10) against the real external AI API. Collect all returned labels. Assert that all N results are identical. Flag and report any divergence. Run with a low-temperature setting if the API supports it to reduce but not eliminate variance.

behaviors.description: The number of tokens consumed for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure mode is that the module fails to pass the token budget constraint (e.g., max_tokens) to the API call, or passes the wrong value. This is local request-construction logic in one module. MOCK-HIDE: Mocking the API client and asserting on call arguments captures this failure correctly — if max_tokens is wrong or absent the test fails. REAL-INFRA: No. LLM-DEP: No — we are checking the module's outbound parameter, not actual consumption by the model. Actual model token consumption is the API's enforcement responsibility; verifying the module correctly delegates that constraint is Unit work.
behaviors.test_description: Configure the module with a specific token budget (e.g., 200 tokens). Mock the external API client. Call classify() with a typical-length document. Assert that the mock was called with max_tokens (or equivalent parameter) set to exactly the configured budget. Optionally parametrize with different budget values to confirm the parameter is not hard-coded.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure mode lives entirely within the module's error-handling branch: it either raises/returns immediately on a rate-limit response or correctly catches it and retries. This is one function's control flow. MOCK-HIDE: Mocking the API to return a rate-limit error (e.g., HTTP 429 or an SDK RateLimitError) on the first call and a valid label on the second call directly exercises the retry path without hiding the failure. REAL-INFRA: No — we do not need a real rate-limited endpoint. LLM-DEP: No. Time.sleep should be patched to avoid slow tests, but the retry logic itself is fully observable in a Unit test.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the external API client so that the first call raises a rate-limit exception and the second call returns a valid taxonomy label. Patch time.sleep (or equivalent wait primitive) to avoid real delays. Call classify() once. Assert it returns the valid label (not an error). Assert that the API mock was called exactly twice, confirming a retry occurred. Assert that sleep was called at least once between the two API calls.
